summary:
Claim: Polishing of composite restorations is challenging due to inherent material composition differences.  
Evidence: - Composites consist of a soft organic matrix (e.g., Bis-GMA/UDMA) prone to abrasion during polishing. - Hard filler particles (e.g., silica) resist wear but leave behind micro-rough surfaces after polishing.  
Answer: A
formatted_summary:

Evidence: - Composites consist of a soft organic matrix (e.g., Bis-GMA/UDMA) prone to abrasion during polishing. - Hard filler particles (e.g., silica) resist wear but leave behind micro-rough surfaces after polishing.  
Answer: A. Answer choice: A
